title.text: Customizing project descriptions with the Content Builder

body:0001.text: This article is machine translated from english

body:0003.text: The Content Builder is a powerful tool for customizing project descriptions. It provides a flexible way to build interactive pages by combining text, images, multimedia, and third-party embeds.

body:0004.text: With the Content Builder you can:

body:0005.text: Create rich layouts using sections and columns

body:0006.text: Add multimedia elements such as videos, presentations, and documents

body:0007.text: Embed surveys and forms for audience interaction

body:0008.text: Collaborate on documents or share updates directly in the project description

body:0009.text: These capabilities help make the engagement process more immersive and user-friendly.

body:0011.text: Add text, images, and embed content

body:0012.text: Include surveys, slides, or news updates before or during engagement

body:0013.text: Create buttons to link between projects or phases

body:0014.text: You can use widgets such as columns to fill in your page creatively (e.g. adding content in the center only, adding an image on the right, ...)

body:0015.text: How to enable the content builder?

body:0016.text: Go to the back-end of your project.

body:0017.text: Click the General tab to access your Project settings

body:0019.text: Toggle Use content builder for description to ON

body:0021.text: How to use the different widgets?

body:0023.text: Locate the left-hand menu with three categories: Sections, Layout, and Content

body:0024.text: Drag any item (e.g., section, layout block) to the canvas in the center of the screen

body:0025.text: Click a block to edit its text, image, or settings; the editor appears on the right side of the screen

body:0026.text: Rearrange or delete blocks as needed

body:0028.text: Content Builder supports a wide range of embeddable content.

body:0029.text: Surveys: Typeform, SurveyMonkey, Qualtrics, and others. Copy the default sharing link and paste it into the embed field.

body:0030.text: Documents: Google Docs/Sheets/Slides, PDFs, and Microsoft Office files via OneDrive are supported. Extract the URL from the embed code if needed.

body:0031.text: Maps: Google Maps and ArcGIS links require cleaning up the embed code to keep only the https URL.

body:0032.text: Data Visualizations: Tableau and Google Data Studio embeds require you to extract and slightly modify the iframe URL.

body:0033.text: Social Posts: Facebook and Eventbrite are supported; Twitter and Instagram are not.

body:0034.text: Videos: YouTube, Vimeo, Dailymotion, Wistia, and Dreambroker (with special URL edit) are supported.

body:0035.text: How to embed third party tools?

body:0036.text: Click the <Embed> option in the left menu

body:0037.text: Get the embed URL from your third-party provider

body:0038.text: Paste the URL into the embed field in Content Builder

body:0039.text: ℹ️ Embedded links may appear differently on desktop and mobile devices. Use the height configuration option to ensure proper display across all devices.

body:0040.text: ⚠️ Always test embeds by opening an incognito browser window to ensure the link works publicly.

body:0042.text: Many embed links need to be trimmed before use.

body:0046.text: Delete everything after the URL (e.g., width= or frameborder=).

body:0050.text: You will get a link that looks like: <iframe width="560" height="315" src="<https://www.youtube.com/embed/abc12>" …>

body:0053.text: Some functions of the Content Builder have restrictions:

body:0054.text: Image resizing isn’t supported; images automatically fill their container.

body:0055.text: Some social platforms (Twitter, Instagram) cannot be embedded due to external policies.

body:0056.text: Embed links often require manual editing to remove iframe code.

body:0058.text: Select the File Attachment widget to upload a file to the project

body:0059.text: ⚠️ 360 input will open in a new window, make sure to save your project beforehand

body:0060.text: Return to the project, in the file attachment widget, select the file you would like to embed

body:0062.text: Right click the attached file in the content builder, select Open Link in New Tab

body:0063.text: Copy the URL in the new tab

body:0064.text: Select the Embed widget, in the website address past the URL from the file attachment tab

body:0065.text: If you do not want the attached file to be visible in the project description:

body:0066.text: Create a new project and hide it by setting the Access Rights to Unlisted

body:0067.text: Attach the PDFs you want to embed in other projects in this project

body:0068.text: Follow the same instructions above for embedding the PDFs
